description: GTAP-grounded tariff component: tariff(t) * Armington * pass_through scaled by a quadratic calibration polynomial in tariff(t). Tariff(t) is interpolated via a trapezoidal stress curve (initial = tariff_current, plateau at tariff_peak, descent back toward tariff_current).
formula_pseudocode: tariff(t) = trapezoid(tariff_current_pct -> tariff_peak_pct, stress_timing); gtap_base_bps = (tariff(t) * armington_elasticity * pass_through) * 100; calib = c0 + c1*tariff(t) + c2*tariff(t)^2; out_bps = gtap_base_bps * calib;

tariff_current_pct.description: Tariff at loan_start_date as decimal (e.g. 0.50 for 50%).

tariff_peak_pct.description: Tariff at peak of stress curve. Equal to tariff_current_pct means flat (no escalation).

armington_elasticity.description: GTAP Armington elasticity for the commodity_gtap sector.

pass_through.description: Share of the tariff passed through to importer purchase price.

source_citations: Armington (1969). A Theory of Demand for Products Distinguished by Place of Production. IMF Staff Papers 16(1).
GTAP 11 Database (Corong et al. 2017). Purdue University. https://www.gtap.agecon.purdue.edu/databases/v11/
Wikipedia summary on Armington elasticity median 3.8 across 3,524 estimates (2019 meta-analysis).
V1 inline CALIBRATION = {constant: -35.9048, linear: 133.6627, quadratic: -116.1008} in SWAPS-1LOAN-WHAT-IF-3-INP-FINAL.json (preserved verbatim by india-us-textiles.json for Iteration 1).
